description:
Recto:
(1) Letter/petition in Judaeo-Arabic from Abū ʿAlī to al-Shaykh al-Makīn. Complains about al-Shaykh al-Mufaḍḍal. Nearly complete, but difficult to read because of the overlapping Arabic script.
(2) Several lines of unidentified Arabic script, difficult to read due to the overlapping Judaeo-Arabic text. 

Verso: 
(1) Draft of a tax receipt for ʿAlī b. ʿImrān al-Yahūdī for his capitation tax (jizya) in New Cairo. The date is difficult to read, possibly 607 AH = 1210/11 CE, though this wouldn't be consistent with the mention of al-Malik al-Kāmil as the sulṭān in the next text block.
(2) Draft of a petition ("al-mamlūk fulān yakhdum al-majlis al-sāmī....") containing various praises and honorifics for the addressee and blessingsfor the prolongation of the reign of the sulṭān al-Malik al-Kāmil. 
(3) On the lefthand page, various additional jottings in Arabic script, difficult to read. One of them refers to ʿĪsā b. ʿAlī al-Mutaṭabbib and "... fī maʿrifat amrāḍ al-ʿayn" — probably a reference to Tadhkirat al-Kaḥḥālīn.There are also a few phrases in Judaeo-Arabic.
description_he: פנים: (1) מכתב/עצומה בערבית-יהודית מאבו עלי אל-שייח' אלמכין. מתלונן על אלשייח' אלמופד'ל. (2) שורות של כתב ערבי לא מזוהה. גב: (1) טיוטת קבלת מס לעלי בן עמראן היהודי לג'יזיה שלו בקהיר החדשה. (2) טיוטת עצומה עם שבחים ותואר כבוד לנמען וברכות להארכת שלטון אלמלך אלכאמל. (3) פתקים שונים.
